page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by Intel processors and built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 128 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 64 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

For high-speed local data access, the m8idb.32xlarge includes 7600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This local storage configuration is integrated directly into the hardware profile, offering high-throughput, low-latency storage operations. Additionally, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps, supporting high-speed data transfers and robust network connectivity without complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This hardware profile represents a highly dense resource allocation suitable for enterprise workloads. The combination of dedicated high-frequency Intel processing cores, substantial memory capacity, and massive local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or caching. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, transactional processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications that demand high network throughput and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture at 3.9 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps. With a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU and massive local storage capacity, the hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive operations. The instance is designed for demanding workloads such as large-scale databases, transactional applications, data warehousing, and general-purpose enterprise systems requiring rapid local storage access and high-speed network throughput.
